prevalence.trend: Stable to increasing due to environmental factors

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME), adapted for EU by EAACI

riskFactors: Exposure to aeroallergens (pollen, dust mites, pet dander)
vulnerablePopulations: Individuals with concomitant asthma or atopic dermatitis
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (sneezing, nasal itching, rhinorrhea, congestion) lasting >1 hour on most days for >1 year, confirmed by positive skin prick test or serum-specific IgE to allergens (ARIA/EAACI guidelines).

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine population screening; symptom-based diagnosis

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Standardized per ARIA/EAACI guidelines across EU member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Stepwise: intranasal corticosteroids as first-line, oral/intranasal antihistamines, allergen immunotherapy for severe cases (EAACI recommendations)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising prevalence from climate-driven pollen increases; access disparities in rural/Eastern EU vs. urban/Western EU
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High availability of over-the-counter treatments and specialized allergy centers in most countries
